svg.text: ASV Foam-and-Deck Catamaran (Size B) · Pool test #1 fixed: Week 9 (Jul 20) · Read left column top→bottom, then right column top→bottom
500mm dowel mast · aluminum L-bracket · sand deck edges
Dry-fit everything before sealing or fastening anything
One coat spray Spar Urethane on all faces + cut edges
Only waterproofing step in the entire build — no reapplication needed
Dries in 1–2hr — noodle lashing (Phase 3) can proceed while it dries
✓ SAME-DAY DRY ONLY — no structural cure gate, proceed to Phase 3 in parallel
Lash Noodle Bundles to Core Rods
Thread 3 noodles per rod, bundle triangularly around the core
Zip-tie lash every ~120mm — 4 ties per rod, trim tails flush
Rod is the structural spine; foam needs zero waterproofing
Position rods parallel at 350mm beam, deck centered between
4× galvanized ½" pipe straps (2/rod) screwed through sealed deck
All mechanical — fully reversible, no cure wait
Aluminum L-bracket screwed to deck at bow center
Dowel mast vertical · TCT40-16R at tip, facing forward
Zip-tie RX coax down mast every ~50mm to enclosure gland
Bare hull in pool/tub · mark actual waterline on each pontoon
Check trim · repeat loaded with ~400g dead weight · verify mast height
Expect ~1.9× buoyancy margin at rest (4.7kg lift vs 2.5kg target)
Mechanical clamp mount onto core rod near stern — no adhesive
Motor leads routed to enclosure gland · ready immediately, no cure
⚠ Stall current (5–8.6A) destroys L298N — firmware trip required first
🔴 BENCH TEST GATE — stall current measured + thrust ≥150g confirmed + firmware trip verified
7× M16 glands (motors×2, JSN, coax, LiPo, UART, spare)
Load electronics · torque glands · IP67 gasket seated all sides
Bolt enclosure to deck with 4× M4 stainless bolts + washers
PV-1/2/3 on AD9226 · buck output 4.9–5.1V · vcgencmd = 0x0
ROS 2 nodes + UART 8-byte packet verified · star ground confirmed
All checks pass → ready for pool test #1 (Week 9, Jul 20)
✅ Hull complete — proceed to pool test #1 (Week 9, Jul 20)
Zero structural cure gates in this design
All hull fasteners are mechanical (zip ties, pipe straps, screws, bolts).
Only passive wait is the same-day deck sealant dry time (Phase 2).
Total build time: ~2 hours hands-on, ~$90–96 in materials.
